Title: NVD Feed - Configure Automatic Feed Update Interval

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed with setup guidance visible.

Step Action: Locate the automatic feed update interval setting.
Step Expected: Interval configuration option is available with documented restrictions.

Step Action: Set the update interval to 3 hours (minimum allowed value).
Step Expected: The value of 3 hours is accepted without error.

Step Action: Save the configuration.
Step Expected: Configuration is saved successfully and a confirmation is displayed.

Step Action: Return to the NVD feed configuration and verify the saved interval.
Step Expected: The interval displays as 3 hours confirming the save persisted.

Step Action: Change the interval to 24 hours (maximum allowed value).
Step Expected: The value of 24 hours is accepted without error.

Step Action: Save the configuration.
Step Expected: Configuration is saved successfully and a confirmation is displayed.

Step Action: Change the interval to an intermediate value (e.g. 12 hours).
Step Expected: The value is accepted and saved successfully.

Step Action: Successfully verified that the automatic NVD feed update interval is configurable between 3 and 24 hours.

Title: NVD Feed - Automatic Feed Interval Cannot Exceed 24 Hours

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the automatic feed update interval setting.
Step Expected: Interval configuration field is present.

Step Action: Attempt to enter an interval value greater than 24 hours (e.g. 25 hours).
Step Expected: The value is entered in the field.

Step Action: Attempt to save the configuration.
Step Expected: A validation error is displayed preventing the save.

Step Action: Verify the error message clearly states the maximum allowed interval is 24 hours.
Step Expected: Error message states the 24-hour maximum restriction.

Step Action: Attempt to enter an interval value less than 3 hours (e.g. 2 hours).
Step Expected: The value is entered in the field.

Step Action: Attempt to save the configuration.
Step Expected: A validation error is displayed preventing the save.

Step Action: Verify the error message clearly states the minimum allowed interval is 3 hours.
Step Expected: Error message states the 3-hour minimum restriction.

Step Action: Successfully verified that the automated NVD feed interval cannot be set below 3 hours or above 24 hours.

Title: NVD Feed - Manual File Upload with Valid File

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the manual NVD file upload or import workflow option.
Step Expected: Manual file import option is accessible.

Step Action: Select a valid expected NVD feed file (correct format and source such as NIST).
Step Expected: File is selected and ready for upload.

Step Action: Specify or confirm the target location or load destination for the file.
Step Expected: Destination is accepted by the system.

Step Action: Initiate the file upload or import process.
Step Expected: Upload process begins without error.

Step Action: Wait for the process to complete.
Step Expected: Process completes successfully.

Step Action: Verify a success message is displayed to the user.
Step Expected: Success confirmation is shown indicating the file was processed.

Step Action: Verify the NVD feed data is updated with the content from the uploaded file.
Step Expected: System reflects updated vulnerability definitions from the uploaded file.

Step Action: Successfully verified that a valid NVD feed file can be manually uploaded and loaded into the system.

Title: NVD Feed - Manual File Upload with Invalid or Unexpected File

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed.

Step Action: Locate the manual NVD file upload or import workflow option.
Step Expected: Manual file import option is accessible.

Step Action: Attempt to upload an unexpected or invalid file (e.g. a .txt file, .jpg, or a malformed feed file).
Step Expected: Invalid file is selected.

Step Action: Initiate the file upload or import process.
Step Expected: Process is initiated.

Step Action: Verify the process fails and does not proceed with the invalid file.
Step Expected: The upload or import process fails.

Step Action: Verify the user is informed of the failure with a clear and descriptive error message.
Step Expected: An error message is displayed indicating the file is invalid or unexpected.

Step Action: Verify the existing NVD feed data is not altered or corrupted by the failed attempt.
Step Expected: Existing NVD data remains intact and unchanged.

Step Action: Successfully verified that an unexpected or invalid file is rejected and the user is informed of the failure.

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.

Step Expected: Guidance text references configuration options and applicable restrictions.

Step Expected: Help topic is accessible from the configuration page or help section.

Step Action: Verify the help topic contains extended guidance beyond what is shown in the UI.
Step Expected: Help documentation provides detailed configuration and usage information.

Title: NVD Feed - No Initial NVD Database Backup Provided

Step Action: Complete a fresh installation of the product.
Step Expected: Installation completes successfully.

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed.

Step Action: Observe whether an initial NVD database backup or pre-loaded vulnerability data is present.
Step Expected: No pre-loaded NVD database or initial backup is present out-of-the-box.

Step Action: Verify the UI or documentation indicates that the initial NVD database is not included with the product.
Step Expected: Guidance or messaging confirms the admin must configure and retrieve the feed independently.

Step Action: Successfully verified that no initial NVD database backup is provided with the product installation.

Title: [REVIEW] NVD Feed - Automatic and Manual Modes Are Mutually Exclusive

Step Action: Login as an Admin user.
Step Expected: Admin dashboard is displayed successfully.

Step Action: Navigate to the NVD feed configuration section in the UI.
Step Expected: NVD feed configuration page is displayed.

Step Action: Enable the Automatic NVD feed update mode.
Step Expected: Automatic mode is enabled.

Step Action: Verify the Manual mode option is no longer selectable or is disabled.
Step Expected: Manual mode option is disabled or unavailable while Automatic mode is active.

Step Action: Switch to Manual mode.
Step Expected: Manual mode is enabled.

Step Action: Verify the Automatic mode option is no longer selectable or is disabled.
Step Expected: Automatic mode is disabled or unavailable while Manual mode is active.

Step Action: Verify a UI control (switch or toggle) exists to toggle between Automatic and Manual modes.
Step Expected: A clearly labeled control exists in the UI to switch between modes.

Step Action: Successfully verified Automatic and Manual NVD feed modes are mutually exclusive. NOTE: This test case is marked for review — the mutual exclusivity behavior is subject to change per requirements.
